title: Falls Prevention for CCRCs & Life Plan Communities

seo.title: Falls Screening for CCRCs & Life Plan Communities | Able Care
seo.description: Objective falls screening that protects your IL feeder, level-of-care transitions and entrance-fee model. Under five minutes per resident.

blocks.backgroundImageAlt: Patient and therapist smiling during a wellness screening session
blocks.headline: Keep residents independent for longer.
blocks.subtitle: For CCRCs and life plan communities. Falls screening that protects your IL feeder, your level-of-care transitions and your entrance-fee model. Under five minutes per resident.

blocks.heading: The IL-to-AL transition problem
blocks.body:
Falls accelerate the move from independent living to assisted living. Each transition costs the life-care fund, reduces entrance-fee revenue and puts pressure on the entire campus model.

Most CCRCs still rely on subjective, paper-based screening tools. These tools miss early decline and leave clinical teams reacting to falls rather than preventing them.

Able Assess replaces subjective observation with four objective, validated metrics. Capture grip strength, sit-to-stand, gait speed and timed up-and-go in a single five-minute session. Risk-stratify every resident on campus, from IL through to skilled nursing.

blocks.heading: How it works across your campus

steps.title: Wellness screen
steps.description: Run a five-minute assessment during a routine wellness visit. Non-clinical staff can operate it with minimal training.

steps.title: Risk stratify
steps.description: Residents are banded by fall risk using normative data. High-risk residents are flagged for clinical review immediately.

steps.title: Intervene early
steps.description: Targeted exercise programs and care plan updates begin before a fall triggers a level-of-care transition.

steps.title: Track campus-wide
steps.description: Longitudinal data across IL, AL and SNF gives leadership a single view of fall risk and functional trends.

blocks.heading: Four metrics, one picture of function

metrics.name: Grip strength
metrics.whyItMatters: Declining grip in IL is one of the earliest signs of functional change. Catch it before it becomes an AL transition.

metrics.name: Sit-to-stand
metrics.whyItMatters: Predicts ability to transfer independently. A key gatekeeper for remaining in independent living.

metrics.name: Gait speed
metrics.whatItMeasures: Walking speed over a short distance.
metrics.whyItMatters: The strongest single predictor of fall risk in older adults. Measurable decline triggers early intervention.

metrics.name: Timed Up and Go
metrics.whyItMatters: Validated falls risk screen in under a minute. Gives clinical teams a defensible, repeatable baseline.

blocks.heading: Residents see their own progress
blocks.imageAlt: Elderly resident holding a GripAble sensor and viewing progress on a tablet screen
blocks.description: Able Assess turns a clinical test into a wellness conversation. Residents view their scores on screen, track improvements over time and engage with programming tied to measurable outcomes.

blocks.heading: Model the impact across your campus
blocks.promoBody: One prevented IL-to-AL transition can offset the annual cost of screening an entire independent living building. See what Able Assess could save your community.

testimonials.quote: It paid for itself in the first quarter. The real win was seeing residents engage with their own wellness data.
Residents can see their own progress on the screen. It turned a clinical test into a conversation about staying strong.

blocks.heading: The numbers that matter to CCRCs

faqs.question: Can wellness staff run the assessment without clinical oversight?
faqs.answer: Yes. Able Assess was designed for non-clinical staff. Wellness directors, fitness coordinators and care aides can all run the five-minute workflow after a short onboarding session.

faqs.question: Does it work across IL, AL and SNF on the same campus?
faqs.answer: Yes. The same platform and metrics are used across all levels of care. This gives leadership a single, consistent view of fall risk and functional trends campus-wide.

faqs.question: How does it protect the life-care fund?
faqs.answer: Falls are the leading trigger for level-of-care transitions. By identifying decline early and intervening before a fall, you reduce unplanned transitions that draw on the life-care fund.

faqs.question: How do we present the data to a non-profit board?
faqs.answer: The dashboard provides population-level reporting that maps directly to quality indicators and financial outcomes. Board-ready summaries can be exported for governance meetings.

faqs.question: How long does deployment take?
faqs.answer: Most communities are live within two weeks. The hardware is a single Bluetooth sensor paired with any tablet. Staff training takes around 15 minutes.

faqs.question: What does it cost?
faqs.answer: Pricing is based on community size and assessment volume. The ROI calculator models payback against reduced falls-related turnover and transition costs. Book a demo to discuss your scenario.

blocks.heading: See Able Assess in a CCRC wellness workflow.
blocks.bodyText: 20 minutes with a clinician who has deployed it across life plan communities.
